docs.title: Podman Desktop + Freelens
docs.text: Podman Desktop manages containers and local Kubernetes clusters. Freelens is a free, open-source GUI for browsing and managing those clusters — no CLI needed.

docs.title: Install Tools
docs.text:
brew install podman-desktop podman helm
brew install --cask freelens

docs.title: 1. Launch and Create a Machine
docs.text:
Open Finder (⌃ + ⌥ + F), go to Applications, and launch Podman Desktop.

On first launch the setup wizard appears. Click Install when prompted — this creates and starts your Podman machine automatically.

docs.title: 2. Install the Kind Extension
docs.text:
1. Click Extensions in the left sidebar.
  2. Search for Kind.
  3. Click the install icon next to it.
  4. Go to the Installed tab and confirm it is active.

docs.title: 3. Create a Kubernetes Cluster
docs.text:
1. Click Kubernetes in the left sidebar.
  2. Click Create new cluster.
  3. Select Kind as the provider.
  4. Give it a name (e.g. dev-cluster) and click Create.

Podman Desktop will spin up the cluster and set it as your active context.

docs.title: 1. Launch Freelens
docs.text: Open Freelens from Applications. It automatically reads your kubeconfig and detects dev-cluster.

docs.title: 2. Browse the Cluster
docs.text: Click on dev-cluster in the sidebar to connect. From here you can browse Workloads, Deployments, Pods, Services, and more — all without touching the terminal.

docs.title: Quick Setup
docs.text:
Already done this before? Just reinstall the tools.

brew install podman-desktop podman helm
brew install --cask freelens

Then launch Podman Desktop to recreate the machine and cluster, and Freelens to connect.
